items.title: Claude Sonnet 4.6 System Card
items.author: anthropic.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has been active with announcements in early 2026. Key model releases include Claude Sonnet 4.6, a balanced model with improved agentic search performance, extended thinking support, and a 1M token context window (beta), and Claude Opus 4.6, which demonstrates notably stronger performance at finding high-severity security vulnerabilities. The company also introduced next-generation Constitutional Classifiers for more efficient universal jailbreak protection, and launched an Automated Al

items.title: Anthropic leak reveals new model 'Claude Mythos' with 'dramatically higher scores on tests' than any previous model - The Decoder
items.author: the-decoder.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has had a busy start to 2026 with major model launches. In February, they released Claude Opus 4.6 (February 5) featuring a 1-million-token context window, stronger coding and planning capabilities, top performance on the Finance Agent benchmark, and extended task completion horizons up to 14.5 hours. Claude Sonnet 4.6 followed on February 17 with broad upgrades across coding, computer use, long-context reasoning, agent planning, and knowledge work — priced the same as Sonnet 4.5. Both

items.title: claude-code CHANGELOG.md - GitHub
items.author: github.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has released several significant API updates in early 2026. Claude Sonnet 4.6 launched as the latest balanced model with improved agentic search performance, fewer token consumption, extended thinking support, and a 1M token context window (beta). Additionally, Claude Haiku 3 has been deprecated with retirement scheduled for April 19, 2026, with migration to Claude Haiku 4.5 recommended. The 1M token context window beta has been expanded to Claude Opus 4.6 alongside existing Sonnet mod

items.title: Alignment Science Blog — Anthropic
items.author: alignment.anthropic.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has published several notable research papers and reports in early 2026. On the AI safety front, the Alignment Science Blog (launched March 23, 2026) features new work including 'The Hot Mess of AI,' which examines how model misalignment scales with intelligence and task complexity, finding that longer reasoning chains lead to incoherence-dominated failures rather than systematic misalignment. Anthropic also published a large-scale empirical analysis of disempowering patterns in real-w

items.title: Details leak on Anthropic's 'step-change' Mythos model - Techzine Global
items.author: www.techzine.eu
items.snippet: Anthropic's most significant Twitter/X announcements in late March 2026 center on the accidental leak and subsequent acknowledgment of a new AI model internally codenamed 'Claude Mythos' (also referred to as 'Capybara'). After a configuration error in Anthropic's content management system exposed nearly 3,000 unpublished assets—including draft blog posts and internal materials—security researchers discovered details about an unreleased model. Anthropic then confirmed via official channels that i
